understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through KA59’s levels by moving the available selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a `+` is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time; when all `+` tiles are covered simultaneously, the level reports WIN, and clearing all levels completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn you spend one step by issuing a CLICK on a grid coordinate. The CLICK targets a destination/interaction cell for the currently selected piece: if a valid path/slide exists under the level’s rules, the selected piece relocates to that clicked tile; if the destination is invalid or blocked by walls/obstacles (or an interaction can’t occur), the piece does not move but the step is still consumed. Movement is constrained by fixed walls/obstacles, and any pushing/blocking behavior depends on whether another object occupies the line of movement (in these turns, attempted moves to [35,17] were blocked). Selection is implicit: actions apply to the currently selected piece, and successive CLICKs move that same piece until a different piece is selected (when present) by clicking it or otherwise switching per the level rules.
